seoTitle: Lima East vs Limestone — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Lima East and Limestone. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Lima East vs Limestone: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lima East and Limestone.
heading: Lima East vs Limestone — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lima East, Victoria and Limestone, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lima East has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Limestone (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lima East or Limestone safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lima East has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Limestone scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lima East scores 33/100 for bushfire risk. Limestone scores 46/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lima East or Limestone?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lima East has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
